steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the passage. The passage describes: (1) past studies hypothesized PSP was homologous (shared ancestor); (2) Aureliano et al. examined common-ancestor fossils and found the bone openings were inconsistent with PSP; (3) the team concluded PSP evolved independently in each clade, contradicting the homology hypothesis.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Aureliano et al. found openings in bones... but the team determined that the sizes of the openings were too varied to be indicative of PSP, thereby weakening the homology hypothesis.': Accurate but incomplete — it only covers one piece of evidence and omits the team's key conclusion that PSP evolved independently. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Although past studies... concluded that PSP... was likely attributable to a common ancestor, those paleontologists lacked access to technology...': The passage never mentions a technology gap between past researchers and Aureliano et al.; this reason is invented. ✗
  • Choice C — '...PSP evolved independently in pterosaurs and was subsequently inherited by the other two clades.': Directly contradicts the passage. The conclusion is that PSP evolved independently in each clade, not that it evolved once and was then inherited. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Aureliano et al. found that pterosaurs and two other Cretaceous clades with PSP likely lacked a common ancestor with the trait, leading the team to posit a different evolutionary origin for PSP than that posited in earlier studies.': Captures both the specific finding (no shared ancestor with the trait) and the broader implication (a different origin story than earlier studies proposed). ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
